events.title: Fourth Crusade and sack of Constantinople
events.description: Crusader forces divert to Constantinople, sack the city and establish the Latin Empire, weakening Byzantine power.

events.title: Genghis Khan proclaimed Great Khan
events.description: Temüjin unites Mongol tribes and is proclaimed Genghis Khan, launching rapid Mongol expansion across Eurasia.

events.title: Delhi Sultanate foundation (Mamluk/Slave dynasty begins)
events.description: Qutb-ud-din Aibak establishes Muslim rule in northern India after Ghurid conquests, beginning the Delhi Sultanate era.

events.title: Magna Carta sealed
events.description: English barons force King John to accept the Magna Carta, limiting royal authority and influencing later constitutional law.

events.title: Mongol invasion of Khwarezmia
events.description: Genghis Khan's forces destroy the Khwarezmian Empire after a trade dispute and massacre of Mongol envoys, opening Persia to Mongol rule.

events.title: Battle of the Kalka River
events.description: Mongol reconnaissance forces defeat a coalition of Rus princes and Cumans, signaling Mongol threat to Eastern Europe.

events.title: Mali Empire rises under Sundiata Keita
events.description: Sundiata Keita consolidates Mandinka polities, founding the Mali Empire which controls trans-Saharan trade routes.

events.title: Mongol invasions of Central Europe (Legnica and Mohi)
events.description: Mongol armies defeat Polish and Hungarian forces at Legnica and Mohi, devastating parts of Central Europe before withdrawing.

events.title: Battle of Köse Dağ
events.description: Mongol forces defeat the Seljuk Sultanate of Rum at Köse Dağ, bringing Anatolia under Mongol influence and weakening Seljuk power.

events.title: Ilkhanate established in Persia
events.description: Hulagu Khan founds the Ilkhanate after campaigns in Persia and the Middle East, reorganizing regional rule under Mongol khans.

events.title: Sack of Baghdad by Mongols
events.description: Mongol forces under Hulagu destroy Baghdad, ending the Abbasid Caliphate's political power and causing massive cultural loss.

events.title: Battle of Ain Jalut
events.description: Mamluk forces defeat the Mongols at Ain Jalut in Palestine, marking the first major Mongol defeat and halting westward expansion.

events.title: Kublai Khan proclaims the Yuan dynasty
events.description: Kublai Khan proclaims the Yuan dynasty in China, consolidating Mongol rule and establishing a new imperial order centered on Beijing.

events.title: Mongol invasions of Japan and the Kamikaze storms
events.description: Kublai Khan's fleets attempt invasions of Japan in 1274 and 1281 but are repelled by Japanese defenders and typhoons (kamikaze).

events.title: Yuan conquest completes; Southern Song falls
events.description: Yuan forces defeat the Southern Song, completing Mongol conquest of China and unifying the country under Yuan rule.

events.title: Sicilian Vespers uprising
events.description: A popular uprising in Sicily expels Angevin rule and triggers the War of the Sicilian Vespers, reshaping Mediterranean politics.

events.title: Mali and West African trade growth (precursor to Mansa Musa)
events.description: Trans-Saharan trade and Islamic scholarship continue to expand in West Africa, setting the stage for Mali's 14th-century prominence under Mansa Musa.

events.title: Mamluk consolidation in Syria and Egypt
events.description: After victories over the Mongols and Crusaders, the Mamluk Sultanate consolidates control over Egypt and Syria, defending Islamic lands.

events.title: Ottoman Beylik founded (Osman I)
events.description: Osman I establishes a small beylik in northwestern Anatolia that will expand into the Ottoman Empire over the following centuries.

events.title: Marco Polo returns to Venice after travels
events.description: Venetian merchant Marco Polo returns from Asia with accounts of the Yuan court and Silk Road trade, influencing European knowledge of Asia.

events.title: Dante Alighieri born (cultural figure)
events.description: Dante, author of the Divine Comedy, writes in Italian and helps shape vernacular literature and European literary culture.

events.title: Angkor and Khmer transitions
events.description: Angkor's political and environmental stresses begin to show; Khmer power faces internal and external pressures though Angkor remains significant.
